dimensionDescriptions.detection_logic: Explain what each prioritized rule or alert is intended to detect, including key thresholds, field dependencies, and time-window assumptions that affect interpretation.
dimensionDescriptions.telemetry_quality: Explain which data sources or fields are missing, delayed, or inconsistent, and justify how those quality limits change analytic confidence and triage reliability.
dimensionDescriptions.false_positive_management: Explain likely noise drivers and justify suppression, allowlisting, or context-enrichment decisions used to reduce non-actionable alert volume.
dimensionDescriptions.false_negative_risk: Explain what attack behavior could be missed after tuning, especially where lower alert volume may conceal stealthy or low-frequency activity.
dimensionDescriptions.tuning_action: Explain the specific tuning lever selected (for example threshold, join/context condition, allowlist/suppression, severity split, or time window) and why it is the most defensible immediate change.
dimensionDescriptions.detection_gap: Explain the most critical coverage or visibility gap and justify why closing this gap should be prioritized for detection quality.
dimensionDescriptions.investigation_pivot: Explain the next investigative check(s), why they are high-yield now, and what result would confirm or challenge your current position.
dimensionDescriptions.mitigation_tradeoff: Explain the operational consequence of the chosen tuning/response action, including implementation burden, business disruption, and residual exposure.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Explain how selected risk level follows from likely impact, exposure, and current evidence quality rather than alert count alone.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Explain how confidence level is justified by corroboration quality, conflicting signals, and telemetry limitations.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_linkage: Explain which concrete evidence items directly support each major conclusion and where inferences remain provisional.
dimensionDescriptions.hypothesis_alignment: Explain how the final hypothesis is supported by your decision path and why competing interpretations are currently less persuasive.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: State what the SIEM is currently showing (signal vs noise), your leading incident interpretation, and your immediate recommendation in stakeholder-ready language.

reportSections.title: Detection Logic Interpretation
reportSections.template: Describe what prioritized rule logic is trying to catch, then explain which thresholds, fields, joins/context, and time windows most influence whether alerts are meaningful.

reportSections.title: Noise vs Signal Rationale
reportSections.template: Justify why selected alerts are treated as higher signal and why deprioritized streams are likely noise or lower-confidence indicators at this stage.

reportSections.title: Telemetry Quality Assessment
reportSections.template: Identify missing/unreliable telemetry (fields, enrichment, source coverage, or ingestion delay) and explain the practical impact on detection precision and incident confidence.

reportSections.title: Tuning Recommendation
reportSections.template: Specify the exact tuning change to implement now, why it is preferable to alternatives, and the expected near-term effect on queue quality and analyst decision speed.

reportSections.title: Tradeoffs and Risks
reportSections.template: Explicitly compare false-positive reduction benefits against false-negative risk, and describe operational side effects or stakeholder impacts of the selected path.

reportSections.title: Next Investigative Pivot
reportSections.template: Name the top 1–2 immediate checks to validate your position, including what outcomes would increase or decrease confidence.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion
reportSections.template: Close with concrete next steps, owners, and trigger conditions for reassessment using concise language suitable for technical and leadership audiences.

gapRules.prompt: Name one specific tuning lever to apply now (threshold, join/context condition, allowlist/suppression, time window, or enrichment dependency) and justify why it is the best immediate control.
Identify at least one telemetry quality issue (missing field, unreliable source, normalization mismatch, or delay) and explain the concrete consequence for detection or triage.
State one explicit post-tuning tradeoff: what operational activity may be impacted and what malicious behavior might be missed as a result.
Specify what you would check next and what finding would materially change your confidence level or chosen response path.
Justify confidence explicitly by linking evidence quality to remaining telemetry limitations and unresolved uncertainty.
